title: satureja alpina subsp. suaveolens
intro: Satureja alpina subsp. suaveolens, also known as the sweet wood savory, is a plant species within the Lamiaceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean and is commonly used for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and reduce stress levels. The sweet wood savory can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine and natural remedies.
